summary:
The summary captures two main points related to why a particular characteristic is false when comparing high copper-silver alloy versus traditional ones—enhanced durability and reduced risk factors like poor tarnishing or corrosion do not apply as stated incorrectly by option B. This maintains relevance while condensing Agent1’s rationale into concise form addressing core issues pertinent to distinguishing features highlighted among dental material types under scrutiny. 
The final answer remains consistent at
\[\boxed{\text{B}}.\] To summarize:

High copper silver alloy exhibits better mechanical properties but doesn't have inherent poor tarnish or corrosion resistance; thus, such an assertion would be inaccurate relative to its known benefits over conventional alternatives. Therefore, the correct choice is \( \boxed{\text{B}} \). 

This encapsulates both critical elements needed without extraneous details.
